issue_title: [security] Public /debug-sentry endpoint can flood production Sentry
issue_body:
## Summary
`/debug-sentry` is reachable without a dev-only guard and intentionally throws inside the production fetch path.

## Evidence / affected paths
- `src/worker.js#L338-L340`
- `src/worker.js#L593-L607`
- `src/worker.js#L612-L620`

`src/worker.js:338-340` unconditionally throws `new Error('Sentry test error')` when `path === '/debug-sentry'`. The catch block at `src/worker.js:593-607` calls `Sentry.captureException(error)` and returns a 500 JSON response. The Sentry wrapper at `src/worker.js:612-620` is enabled whenever `env.SENTRY_DSN` is set. `isDev` is computed at line 278 and used for other behavior, but not for this debug route.

## Impact
Anonymous clients can deliberately generate Sentry events and 500s, consuming quota/cost and drowning real production errors in noise. The endpoint appears to serve only Sentry wiring verification.

## Proposed fix
Gate `/debug-sentry` behind `isDev` or require an authenticated/debug-token header. In production, return 404/403 without throwing or calling Sentry.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Non-dev `GET /debug-sentry` returns 404/403 and does not call `Sentry.captureException`.
- [ ] Dev/local `GET /debug-sentry` still verifies Sentry wiring if that workflow is required.
- [ ] A regression test covers production non-throw behavior.
- [ ] Real unexpected exceptions elsewhere are still captured by Sentry.

## Source
DoriDori Issue Scout + OpenCode session/title: ses_1cac512d0ffec7644mHdbZo9AL / DoriDori simpletool-app issue scout: debug endpoint verification
